name: Grav Flat-File CMS REST API
what_it_does: Open-source flat-file PHP CMS with optional REST API — enabling developers to build fast websites without a database, storing content in Markdown files with YAML front matter, with a plugin ecosystem, Twig-based templating, page hierarchy, and optional headless delivery via the Grav REST API plugin.
use_cases: Delivering headless content via Grav REST API plugin for decoupled frontend applications
Managing Grav pages and assets via API for content migration and automation
Querying Grav site content via REST API for static site generation workflows
Managing Grav user accounts via API for CMS user provisioning
Building Grav plugin-based custom API endpoints for specialized content workflows
not_for: High-traffic sites with thousands of pages (flat-file scanning degrades at scale)
Teams needing database-backed content with complex queries
Non-PHP developers (Grav is PHP — Twig templating knowledge required)
best_when: A PHP developer or agency needs a fast, database-free CMS for small to medium websites — where content lives in Git-trackable Markdown files, and a simple plugin-based ecosystem handles 90% of requirements.
avoid_when: You have large content volumes (1000+ pages), need complex content relationships, or want managed SaaS CMS.

auth.notes: REST API via Grav REST API plugin (official Grav plugin). API key header auth. No OAuth2. No webhooks. No built-in REST API — requires plugin installation. Grav Admin API for CMS management. PHP session auth for admin panel. No fine-grained scopes. Self-hosted — user controls all auth.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (MIT license)
pricing.notes: Open source project. Created by Andy Miller (RocketTheme) in 2014. MIT license. GitHub: getgrav/grav (14,000+ stars). No database — content in Markdown files + YAML. Written in PHP 7.4+. Twig templating. Plugin ecosystem (1,000+ plugins). GPM (Grav Package Manager). Used by agencies, portfolio sites, documentation sites. Very fast — file-based caching. Markdown-based content authoring. Admin panel plugin for non-technical editors. REST API via plugin. Popular in academic and documentation communities.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API via plugin — must install separately. No webhooks. API key auth. Open source. PHP expertise required. Self-hosted. No rate limits. Flat-file model degrades with large content sets. Plugin ecosystem quality varies.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Page reads idempotent by route/slug. Content reads idempotent. File system-based — reads are idempotent. Write operations idempotent for same content path.
known_agent_gotchas: No built-in REST API — must install Grav REST API plugin
No built-in HTTP webhooks — use custom plugin or webhook plugin
Flat-file scanning degrades with large content sets (500+ pages)
Plugin ecosystem quality varies — evaluate plugins before production use
PHP expertise required — Twig templating knowledge needed for customization

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS depends on server config. User-controlled data residency. API key via plugin. PHP session auth. No OAuth2. Open source — auditable. Flat-file content means Git-trackable security audit trail. User responsible for server hardening. Plugin security depends on plugin quality. No compliance certifications.
